en.name: End tidal carbon dioxide concentration
en.shortDescription: End-tidal CO2 (EtCO2) — exhaled CO2 at end of breath, as partial pressure (mmHg/kPa) or concentration (%).
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context End-tidal carbon dioxide (EtCO2) is the concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas a patient exhales at the very end of a breath, measured continuously by a capnograph on the breathing circuit. It is the standard non-invasive surrogate for arterial CO2 and is used to confirm correct tube placement, monitor ventilation, and detect changes in cardiac output. In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp. It is recorded in intensive care, anaesthesia, and emergency settings for ventilated patients. EtCO2 is reported either as a **partial pressure** (PetCO2, in **mmHg** or kPa) or as a **volume fraction / concentration** (in **%**); the two are not interchangeable by a fixed factor, because the partial pressure is the fraction multiplied by the barometric pressure [1]. ## Included Concepts The set groups the SNOMED and LOINC concepts for end-tidal CO2 by how the value is expressed. ### Generic (default) - *4353938 / 250784008 — End tidal carbon dioxide concentration (SNOMED)*: the general concept; the default when the source does not distinguish partial pressure from volume fraction. ### As a partial pressure (PetCO2) Reported in **mmHg** (or kPa). This is the usual ICU/anaesthesia representation. - *3035357 / 19891-1 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Exhaled gas --at end expiration (LOINC)*: measured PetCO2. - *3035115 / 19890-3 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Exhaled gas by Calculated --at end expiration (LOINC)*: calculated PetCO2. ### As a volume fraction / concentration Reported in **%**. - *3012287 / 19888-7 — Carbon dioxide/Gas.total.at end expiration in Exhaled gas by Infrared absorption (LOINC)*: by infrared capnography (the standard method). - *3017485 / 19889-5 — Carbon dioxide/Gas.total.at end expiration in Exhaled gas (LOINC)*: method not specified. **Note on units.** Partial-pressure concepts use **`8876 / mm[Hg] — millimeter mercury column`** (with a registered mmHg↔kPa conversion: 1 mmHg = 0.133 kPa, 1 kPa = 7.50 mmHg); volume-fraction concepts use **`8554 / % — percent`**. The generic SNOMED concept is recommended in mmHg (the dominant ICU representation). ## Mapping Notes - **Partial pressure vs concentration are not a fixed conversion**: PetCO2 (mmHg) is the end-tidal CO2 fraction (%) multiplied by the barometric pressure, so the mmHg↔% relationship depends on barometric pressure and is *not* a constant factor [1]. Do not convert between the % concepts and the mmHg concepts with a fixed multiplier — map each value to the concept matching its reported unit. - **Default concept** when the source does not specify whether EtCO2 is a partial pressure or a fraction: *4353938 / 250784008 — End tidal carbon dioxide concentration*. When the unit is known, prefer the matching LOINC concept (mmHg → partial-pressure concept; % → volume-fraction concept). - **mmHg vs kPa**: both are partial-pressure units; a conversion is registered (1 kPa = 7.50 mmHg). Map a kPa value to a partial-pressure concept and convert as needed. - **Not arterial CO2**: arterial CO2 partial pressure (PaCO2, from a blood gas) is a different measurement in its own concept set; EtCO2 is the exhaled-gas value. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 19890-3 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Exhaled gas by Calculated --at end expiration. Available: https://loinc.org/19890-3/

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Fix name typo (carbone dioxyde -> carbon dioxide). Expand to cover EtCO2 as partial pressure (PetCO2, LOINC 19891-1/19890-3) and as volume fraction (LOINC 19888-7/19889-5) alongside the generic SNOMED concept. Registered recommended units (mmHg for partial pressure, % for fraction) and mmHg<->kPa conversions. Resolves to 5. Added clinical description.
